source: data/espncricinfo/Ball by Ball Commentary & Live Score - ENG vs IND,England Batting 3rd T20I.pdf

balls.commentary: Length ball in the fourth-stump channel, straightens a touch. Steps out of his crease and across to the off side, and defends towards mid-on 2.29pm Here we go then. Jason Roy and Jos Buttler will open again, no doubt relieved to look around the field and see no sign of Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Who takes the new ball then? It's going to be Avesh Khan. One wide-ish slip in place. Fine leg and third man are back. 2.20pm Thanks, Andrew. Interesting selections all round. With Hardik Pandya rested, India's entirely new-look attack will have to deal with the pressure of knowing each of the five bowlers will most likely have to send down four overs each -on a ground that often produces run-fests in white-ball cricket, and against a power-packed England line-up. team is that barring Jadeja, none of the batsmen can bowl and none of the bowlers is a decent batsman." --Avesh Khan can tonk a few, to be fair. And Shreyas Iyer can bowl... sort of.
Length ball in the corridor, opens his bat face and stabs towards backward point
Goes wide on the crease and angles a length ball into the stumps. Looks to flick, hits it one-bounce to Karthik at short midwicket. Bounced just a
Short outside off, stops on the pitch a little, and Roy flat-bats it, tennis-style, into the gap between short midwicket and mid-on. Doesn't really middle it
Quick, hard length in the corridor. Roy looks to make room and slap through the covers, and he's beaten by a slight lack of bounce
Slower offcutter in the corridor, back of a length, opens the bat face and steers to backward point

Ooh, good ball. Pitches on a similar spot to the previous one, and Buttler looks to ride the bounce and punch it again, but it seams away from him and beats his outside edge. Pant fluffs the grab behind the stumps

Back of a length again, pitches in the corridor outside off and on this occasion it cuts back in sharply. Not quite short enough to pull, and it sneaks below his bat and smacks into his body, before rolling into the off side for a leg bye
Back of a length, angling in towards off stump, lets it come on and tucks it to the right of midwicket

Wow. Just a touch short outside off, and Buttler is in position so quickly to pull well in front of square, sees it so well that he's able to get a full arm extension in and clear the midwicket boundary
Lovely. Bit of a gift, though, a half-volley outside off stump, and Buttler
Skiddy. Just short of a length, close to off stump, beats Roy with zip off the pitch as he looks to punch through the covers Spin in just the third over. Here's Ravi Bishnoi.
A bit slower and wider, gets forward for a bottom-handed drive to mid-off
Back of a length, doesn't turn a great deal after initially seeming to angle down leg. Cramped for room on a half-kneeling pull, plays it without a lot of power to short fine leg
Quickish, close to off stump, Roy makes room and cuts crisply, but JAdeja dives right at backward point and keeps him down to a single
Flat length ball sliding on towards leg stump, lets it come and clips to deep midwicket

Shot! A bit of relief for Roy to connect so sweetly after being 4 off 11 balls. Not a rank bad ball, just floated slightly too full while angling in towards middle and leg, and he clears his front leg and hoicks it

Miscued, but safe. Length ball just outside off, and Buttler looks to get on the front foot and hit over mid-off. Gets a little too much underneath the ball and scoops it in the air, but safely clears the fielder running back Avesh changes ends.
Back of a length, angling into the body, sneaks under the short-arm pull and rolls to the left of the keeper off the body
Back of a length, a bit of nip in towards middle and leg, clipped watchfully to midwicket
Bowled him. Was that a change of pace? 123kph, so it probably was. Not easy to pick. Length ball outside off, and Buttler looks to play the flat-
Length ball slanting across, and another inside-edge. Looks to swipe it through midwicket, and bottom-edges into his pads Stays over the wicket to the left-handed Dawid Malan.
Another slower ball, slanting across into the corridor, pitching just short of a length. Looks to stand tall and punch into the covers, and he's beaten by the lack of pace

Decent delivery, on a length, just outside off, and he frees his arms and launches it straight back over the bowler's head India bring on their second spinner inside the powerplay. Here's Jadeja. Left-arm around to Roy.

Quicker length ball, slightly closer to off stump, defended into the covers Looks like we'll have a delay as they hunt for the ball, which seems to have hidden itself somewhere in the vicinity of the sightscreen. getting a chance today... he bowled so well in 1st T20I"
Shovelled. With precision. Flat, angling in towards the stumps, and he extends his arms through a jab with barely any follow-through, lifts the ball over mid-on
Flat, back of a length, close to off stump, chopped to backward point
Once again, doesn't quite get the width he needs to place the cut past backward point. Hits it straight to the fielder
Back of a length, keeps going with the arm towards the stumps, cramped while looking for room, punches to extra-cover
Length ball angling in towards off stump, clipped away through square leg Here's Harshal Patel. Five bowlers in the first six overs.

Slants a length ball into a wide-ish line, as expected, with that sweeper back, and Malan frees his arms and looks to drive over extra-cover, I think. Ends up slicing the ball much finer than intended, over backward point and all the way for four Stays over the wicket to Malan. Deep cover point is on the boundary.
Dropped! Slower ball on leg stump, and Malan is deceived by it. Looks to flick and ends up popping it straight
Fullish on the pads, clipped down to the left of deep square leg

Another slower ball miscued in the air, but this time it falls safely. Length ball, angling away from off stump. No feet from Malan, looks to reach out and drive through the covers, then checks his shot. Spoons it in the air, but it doesn't carry as far as mid-off
Back of a length, angling into the stumps, Roy plays a short-arm jab out to deep square leg
Length ball, a little slower, on the stumps, gets down low and reverse-pulls to deep point

Misfield on the point boundary, from Kohli of all people. Makes good ground to his left after Malan slaps one off the back foot through the covers, dives, but can't stop it despite the ball being within his reach
Flat length ball angling across to off stump, hangs back and punches to sweeper cover

Big appeal for lbw as Roy misses the sweep and is hit on the back leg. Not given. Was he struck outside the line of off stump? India seem to think so. They think of a review but decide against it. Wrong'un from Bishnoi, length ball, and Roy misses his sweep and is hit on the back thigh. Seemed to strike just outside the line
Bishnoi is doing well to keep denying Roy width on the cut. Chops it square

And there's the wicket! Back of a length outside off stump, and it's quick and there's extra bounce. Goes hard at the cut, and only manages a top-edge to Pant Jason Roy c †Pant b Umran Malik 27 26b 1x4 2x6 36m) SR 103.84 Here's Malik again.

And what a way to begin! Full, angling into the stumps, and he's in the perfect position to bring his bat down dead-straight and play the perfect straight drive, between the stumps and the non-striker Phil Salt, for the first time in this series. powerplay. He needs more fielder outside of the inner circle. Because of his pace, the ball travels fast after being hit. " --He tends to concede a lot of boundaries off edges behind the wicket too.

Back of a length close to off stump. Looks for the back-foot punch, plays a touch early, and spoons it briefly in the air but it bounces well short of mid-off, who fumbles a routine stop to allow the single 7.3 1w UMRAN MALIK TO SALT, 1 WIDE
Back of a length outside off, chopped to backward point. 142kph Round the wicket to Malan.

Shot. Malik looks to dig this short, angling into the body, but it's only (for him) 141kph, and it only gets up to chest height, and Malan is a very good puller. Easily beats the deep square leg sweeper to his right
Length ball close to off stump, defended towards mid-off
The odd ball has been stopping today, and this one, a length ball going with the angle towards leg
Bam. Length ball finishing within the stumps, and that's a super shot .Gets a good stride in and slog-sweeps with great power over the backward square leg boundary Stays around the wicket to Malan.
A bit slower and wider, looks to drive through the covers and inside-edges into the ground and then onto his pads Now Jadeja goes over the wicket. Looks like we might have lost this ball too.
Length ball outside off, driven towards sweeper cover
Yikes, that bounces just a foot or so short of short third man. Shortish outside off, turns in and cramps him on the cut. Hits it in the air, and it bounces just short of the fielder
Length ball close to off stump, punched to long-off Back round the wicket to Roy.

Fullish, angling into middle and leg, gets his front leg out of the way and clips to long-on, one-bounce Harshal comes back into the attack. Vishal Pawar : "Wasn't someone like Tewatia a better choice than the left arm spinners we have in the current squad? " --While Tewatia's batting has levelled up, he only bowled six overs during IPL 2022 and went at 12 an over.

Full on the pads, slower ball, clipped towards long leg Harshal stays over the wicket to Malan. "Pretty much Malan's dream attack to face," notes Matt Roller. "One of England's better players of high pace, and a left-arm spinner turning the ball into his arc."
The dipping slower yorker, and Salt plays all over and all around it. Superb stuff. A low full-toss rather than the yorker if you want to be
Full length, steps to the off-side and nudged to leg Greetings. India are turning the screw once more ... And with that, it's back to Andrew Miller for the rest of this innings.
Slower ball, on the pads, nudged to the on-side
Slow bouncer, and that's exceptionally disguised, a pre-meditated duck as Livingstone picked the length, but not the pace as the ball hopped over his shoulder
Over the wicket, another googly out of Malan's arc, he can't reach his cut
Googly again, and another flat-footed waft as the ball spins away once more
Solid contact on the cut this time, but only as far as the tumbling sweeper
Free hit... fired out so wide, Malan chases it across his crease and scuffs a pull to midwicket
Steps across his stumps early, and heaves a slog-sweep over fine leg! A straighter line on the legbreak, and a no-ball too...
Googly, turning into the pads, nudged to the on-side
Toe-ended on the lofted drive, hitting through the googly, over wide long-off, and scooped up in one hand on the rope
Outside off, Malan sets himself with a firm base, and clubs the cut hard through extra cover. Shot Malan vs Jadeja...
Pressed with a high elbow into the covers
Cracked at the top of the bounce, out to the cover sweeper
Attempted reverse sweep, but fired outside off, and can't connect
Flogged on the up to the on-side, but not timed as Axar at wide long-on gathers on the bounce
Outside off, full length, fired through to the keeper as Malan can't lay back on ball Here's Malik. Now then England are ticking along ... but this innings does feel a gear lower than it needs to be
Round the wicket, driven firmly on the up, into the covers, and Malan has his fifty, from 30 balls.
Sits up, back of a length, and clubbed in front of square! Rocking
Opens the face and launched high into the short boundary! Full length, angled into the toes, and this is the over that England needed to kick up a gear
Battered through the line, pace off a touch that time, and a sprint to the non-strikers' end as mid-off fumbles
On the legs, picked up with the pace but straight at backward square. A fumble on the bounce, but well enough in the way of the boundary
Make that 1 for 12 off 2.1! Stand and deliver from Livingstone, a swipe from a strong base, hard through midwicket! Avesh is back. 1 for 6 from two overs so far. Can't argue with that!
Flicked off the legs, pace off this time, and deflected into the gap behind square
Attempted yorker, inside-edged on the full into the front pad
Chopped to the off-side

Hacked into the gap at third man! A fat outside edge, loops over the head of the man backpedalling towards the rope Bharat : "Scoring at 10 rpo and you feel it's a gear down huh? Why can't you be atleast try to look like you are not biased?" The average score at Trent Bridge is 191, and this India team is stacked with run-makers. I am simply speaking in facts
Low full toss, and that has to travel! A simple pick-up off the pads, fast and flat, over the head of the man set back at wide long-on!
Chopped to the off side Six overs to go... ten an over is 202... England probably need more than that, to be honest
A step to the off-side, and deposited! Malan is in the mood now! Jadeja throws it out wide, but Malan just shimmies across his
A drag-down, biffed off the back foot to long-off, and there's the 150
Round the wicket, beats a flat thrash as Jadeja turns it a touch from a tight line 14.4 1w JADEJA TO LIVINGSTONE, 1 WIDE Fired out wide, attempted cut, and misses out

Wider line, Livingstone snatches at the cut and misses out, he's getting frustrated history, and how other batters are going about, can't help but think that Roy played a hugely detrimental innings up at the top (feels weird even saying this, lol) " Odd isn't it... but it wasn't his most fluent
Excellent finish to the over, nothing more than a steered cut to deep point
Bouncer, cloths the pull, into no-man's land at short midwicket, and they scamper through

Fires in the slower ball, which dips just past the off stump as Livingstone swings too early. He's not quite found his range yet... Sanket : "There are people who are pessimists to the core and suck joy out of life by goading people beyond reasonable limts. I think the commentators are the same." If you say so!
That'll help the cause! A knee-high full-toss, shaping into the pads, and nothing more than a flick of the wrists over fine leg!
That's wasteful bowling and another vast blow! Another leg-sided full-toss, dispatched over fine leg again! Wrists of steel in that stroke! And out comes a replacement ball
Good riposte, banged in short, but it's a slower ball, Pant fumbles as the ball dies in front of him
Outside off, climbing past the cut as Livingstone shuffles to the off side
Drags it down, saw the batter coming, and they steal the single as the ball thuds off Livi's pads

Steps to the off side, and slogged straight up in the air! Pant's only concern is to avoid tripping over the stumps as he steadies himself to swallow a top-edge. But that's the end of a fantastic knock from Malan. 27 from his last nine balls, after a 30 ball fifty Dawid Malan c †Pant b Ravi Bishnoi 77 39b 6x4 5x6 65m) SR 197.43
Scuffed pull, out to the leg side, this is a fine over at a key moment for India
A massive wipe at the googly, and a looping leading edge to point! This is a staggering good over from Bishnoi.

Outside off, chopped hard at point, and that's a two-run over, when England would have been targeting two a ball at least. Huge effort Praveen Kumar: "Over 1016 score roared from 86167 is 81 in 6 overs. Same runrate gives 54 in the next four and take them to 221. If thats not sufficient on any surface then what is?" Weird stuff happens at Trent Bridge... and on this evidence, 221 is riches!
Back of a length, leg-stump line, Brook swings hard into the pull in front of square leg. Good start from the new man
Free hit... Brook keeps a firm base, flogs high to wide long-on, a
Cheeky dab through third man! Opens the face at the last minute, initially planning to launch to leg, but tapping the four instead, and it's a no-ball too
Speared in on the full length, flicked to the on-side
Back of a length, pulled to wide long-on, unleashing his levers into the stroke
Wide yorker, and that's a huge drilled drive over the covers! Livingstone unleashes his forearms and clears the ropes by a distance
Good slower ball, hops into the midriff as they scamper the leg bye for england, too many dot balls." He has the power to atone...
A round-house smash to deep midwicket, and dropped by Kohli! He was hurtling at the ball, and the ball
Flashed out to deep midwicket, and lots of ironic cheers as Kohli gathers this one safely enough
Slower ball in the blockhole, and boshed! Brook is waiting for it as it arrives, and one bounce through the off side
Slow bouncer, gripping the pitch and eluding an uppercut

Big top-edge, pocketed by Bishnoi at deep midwicket! Brook climbs into the pull, but it's high on his bat as the bowler cramps his intentions on the angle. Still, a handy cameo to keep England on course for that 220-ish total Harry Brook c Ravi Bishnoi b Patel 19 9b 3x4 0x6 10m) SR 211.11
Walks to the off-side, rolls the wrists on the pull, picks off the single to keep the strike

Bashed through the line, just back of a good length, high on Livingstone's bat but Jordan is eager to make it two as long-off gathers 19.1 1w UMRAN MALIK TO LIVINGSTONE, 1 WIDE Short and very wide, too wide to chase that Livingstone faced 14 balls from Umran Malik in the IPL this year and hit him for 31 runs He's got six more potentially now...  214  324
Fired into the pads, and a fine tumbling stop from Iyer to cut off the boundary at backward square A break in play for a lady fanning herself behind the bowler's arm... it is hot out there
Good hard full length, scuffed into the off side and Livingstone is off-strike
They all count! Very full length from Malik, Jordan is beaten for pace but a fat inside-edge skids past short backward square
Hoisted over the short boundary! Jordan opens his stance, opens his face, slices it up and over extra cover!
